title: Auto-configuration Internals
summary: How @SpringBootApplication triggers auto-configuration, condition annotations, spring.factories, and the AutoConfiguration import selector.

sections.title: Auto-configuration Bootstrap Process
sections.content:
sequenceDiagram
    participant App as SpringApplication.run()
    participant AC as AutoConfigurationImportSelector
    participant SF as spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports
    participant Cond as @Conditional evaluators
    participant Ctx as ApplicationContext
    App->>AC: load auto-configs
    AC->>SF: read all registered auto-config classes
    SF-->>AC: 150+ AutoConfiguration classes
    AC->>Cond: evaluate @Conditional on each
    Cond-->>AC: include / exclude
    AC->>Ctx: register matching beans
    Ctx-->>App: fully configured context

sections.title: Condition Evaluation Chain
sections.content:
graph TD
    A[DataSourceAutoConfiguration] --> B{@ConditionalOnClass\nDataSource.class}
    B -->|not found| X1[SKIP]
    B -->|found| C{@ConditionalOnMissingBean\nDataSource}
    C -->|bean exists| X2[SKIP - user defined their own]
    C -->|no bean| D{@ConditionalOnProperty\nspring.datasource.url}
    D -->|not set| X3[SKIP]
    D -->|set| E[Create DataSource Bean ✓]
    style E fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style X1 fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style X2 fill:#dc2626,color:#fff

sections.title: Auto-configuration ordering matters
sections.content: Use @AutoConfigureBefore, @AutoConfigureAfter, or @AutoConfigureOrder to control when your auto-configuration runs relative to others. For example, @AutoConfigureAfter(DataSourceAutoConfiguration.class) ensures the DataSource bean exists before yours runs.

## Debugging Auto-configuration

```bash
# See which auto-configs were applied and why
java -jar app.jar --debug

# Or in application.properties
debug=true
```

This prints the **auto-configuration report**:
- **Positive matches** — configs that were applied
- **Negative matches** — configs that were skipped and why
- **Exclusions** — explicitly excluded

Also use the Actuator endpoint:
```
GET /actuator/conditions
```

sections.question: What does @ConditionalOnMissingBean achieve in an auto-configuration class?
options: It creates a bean only when the application is missing its main class
It allows users to override the auto-configured bean by defining their own
It prevents the bean from being created if a bean of any type is missing
It skips auto-configuration entirely if no beans are found
sections.explanation: @ConditionalOnMissingBean means 'only create this bean if the user hasn't already defined one of this type.' This is the override mechanism — if you define your own DataSource bean, Spring Boot's auto-configured one won't be created, giving you full control.
